react.summary: React SPA — compile to static assets and serve from any CDN or static host. No server runtime required at runtime.

hostingOptions.name: Vercel
notes: Connect your Git repo and Vercel auto-detects Vite/CRA.
Zero-config HTTPS, global CDN, and preview URLs per PR.

hostingOptions.name: Netlify
notes: Add a _redirects file with `/* /index.html 200` for client-side routing.

hostingOptions.name: Cloudflare Pages
notes: Fastest global edge network. Add _redirects for SPA routing.

hostingOptions.name: GitHub Pages
notes: Set `base` in vite.config.ts to match the repo name.
Use the peaceiris/actions-gh-pages action to deploy from CI.

hostingOptions.name: AWS S3 + CloudFront
notes: Run: aws s3 sync dist/ s3://<bucket> --delete
Invalidate CloudFront: aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*'

envVars.name: VITE_API_URL
envVars.description: Base URL for the backend API

dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy React App
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

checklist: Ensure all environment variables are prefixed with VITE_ (for Vite projects)
Configure client-side routing fallback (/* → /index.html) on your host
Enable HTTPS — all major static hosts provide it for free
Set cache-control headers: long TTL for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html
Run `npm run build` locally first and verify the dist/ output

nextjs.summary: Next.js app — supports SSR, SSG, and edge runtimes. Vercel is the zero-config option; Docker enables self-hosting on any cloud.

hostingOptions.name: Vercel
notes: Made by the Next.js team — zero configuration required.
App Router, Server Components, ISR, and Edge functions all work natively.
Connect your Git repo and every push auto-deploys.

hostingOptions.name: Railway
notes: Set PORT env var to $PORT (Railway injects it automatically).
Add `output: 'standalone'` to next.config.ts for smaller Docker images.

hostingOptions.name: Fly.io
notes: Run `fly launch` then `fly deploy`.
Use standalone output mode for the smallest possible image.

hostingOptions.name: AWS Amplify
notes: Supports SSR via Lambda@Edge. Enable SSR in the Amplify console.
Requires Node.js 18+ runtime setting.

hostingOptions.name: Render
notes: Deploy as a Web Service. Free tier spins down after inactivity.

envVars.name: NEXTAUTH_SECRET
envVars.description: Required if using NextAuth.js — generate with: openssl rand -base64 32

envVars.name: NEXTAUTH_URL
envVars.description: Canonical URL of your deployment (required for NextAuth.js)

envVars.name: DATABASE_URL
envVars.description: Postgres/MySQL connection string if the app uses a database

dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
# next.config.ts: output: 'standalone'  ← required
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy Next.js App
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
        env:
          NODE_ENV: production
      # Add your deploy step here (vercel deploy, fly deploy, etc.)

checklist: Add `output: 'standalone'` to next.config.ts if self-hosting with Docker
Set NODE_ENV=production before running `next build`
Configure all NEXT_PUBLIC_* env vars in your host's dashboard
Run database migrations before the first deployment if the app uses a DB
Verify ISR revalidation works — some hosts require additional config
Enable HTTP/2 on your reverse proxy (nginx/caddy) for optimal performance

astro.summary: Astro site — defaults to static output (zero JS by default). For SSR, install an Astro adapter matching your host. Cloudflare Pages is ideal for edge SSR; any static host works for static output.

hostingOptions.name: Cloudflare Pages
notes: For SSR: install @astrojs/cloudflare adapter and set output: 'server'.
Static output: no adapter needed — just connect your repo.
Use `wrangler pages deploy` for CLI deployments.

hostingOptions.name: Vercel
notes: For SSR: install @astrojs/vercel adapter.
Static output works without any adapter.

hostingOptions.name: Netlify
notes: For SSR: install @astrojs/netlify adapter.
Add a netlify.toml with [[redirects]] if using SSR.

hostingOptions.name: GitHub Pages
notes: Set `site` and `base` in astro.config.mjs.
Use withastro/action GitHub Action for automated deploys.
Static output only — SSR requires a different host.

hostingOptions.name: Railway
notes: For SSR: install @astrojs/node adapter with mode: 'standalone'.
Set PORT and HOST environment variables.

envVars.name: PUBLIC_API_URL
envVars.description: Client-accessible API base URL (prefix PUBLIC_ for Astro env exposure)

dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
# SSR mode — requires @astrojs/node adapter with mode: 'standalone'
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0 PORT=4321
EXPOSE 4321
CMD ["node", "./dist/server/entry.mjs"]

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy Astro Site
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      # Static: upload dist/ to your host
      # SSR: build Docker image and push

checklist: Choose your output mode: 'static' (default), 'server' (SSR), or 'hybrid'
Install the correct adapter for your host (@astrojs/cloudflare, @astrojs/vercel, @astrojs/node, etc.)
For static: verify all pages are pre-rendered with `astro build --verbose`
Prefix client-accessible env vars with PUBLIC_ (Astro requirement)
Set `site` and `base` in astro.config.mjs if deploying to a sub-path
For SSR: set HOST=0.0.0.0 so the server binds to all interfaces in production

express.summary: Express API/server — deploy as a persistent Node.js process. Railway and Fly.io have the easiest DX; Docker gives you full portability.

hostingOptions.name: Railway
notes: Railway injects PORT automatically — read it with process.env.PORT.
Connect your GitHub repo; every push triggers a deploy.
Add a PostgreSQL or Redis service with one click.

hostingOptions.name: Fly.io
notes: Run `fly launch` to auto-generate fly.toml, then `fly deploy`.
Fly provisions a free TLS certificate and global anycast IPs.

hostingOptions.name: Render
notes: Free tier spins down after 15 minutes of inactivity — upgrade to keep it alive.
Managed PostgreSQL and Redis available as add-ons.

hostingOptions.name: DigitalOcean App Platform
notes: Set HTTP port to match your Express app's PORT env var.
Managed databases available in the same project.

hostingOptions.name: AWS EC2 + Elastic Beanstalk
notes: Run `eb init` then `eb deploy`.
Elastic Beanstalk handles load balancing and auto-scaling.

envVars.name: PORT
envVars.description: Port the Express server listens on

envVars.name: NODE_ENV
envVars.description: Set to 'production' to disable dev middleware

envVars.name: SESSION_SECRET
envVars.description: Secret for signing session cookies — at least 32 chars

dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy Express App
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build-and-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Build Docker image
        run: docker build -t my-express-app .
      # Add: docker push + deploy step for your host

checklist: Read PORT from process.env.PORT (not hardcoded) — all hosts inject it
Set NODE_ENV=production before starting in production
Configure CORS to only allow your frontend's origin — never use * in production
Add a health-check endpoint (e.g. GET /healthz returning 200) for load balancers
Use a process manager (PM2) or systemd if running on a raw VM
Secure secrets in environment variables — never commit .env to source control
Set up database connection pooling (e.g. PgBouncer) for high-traffic APIs

laravel.summary: Laravel PHP application — Laravel Forge manages production VPS servers with zero-downtime deploys. Vapor provides serverless AWS deployment. Docker enables self-hosting on any cloud.

hostingOptions.name: Laravel Forge
notes: Provisions and manages VPS servers on DigitalOcean, AWS, Linode, or Vultr.
Zero-downtime deployment via atomic releases.
Automated SSL (Let's Encrypt), queue workers, and schedulers.

hostingOptions.name: Laravel Vapor
notes: Serverless deployment on AWS Lambda — scales to zero.
Requires Vapor subscription and AWS account.
Run `vapor deploy production` to deploy.
Queues use SQS; sessions/cache use DynamoDB or ElastiCache.

hostingOptions.name: Railway
notes: Deploy via Docker for full PHP control.
Add a MySQL or Postgres service to the same Railway project.
Run migrations in the deploy command: `php artisan migrate --force`.

hostingOptions.name: Render
notes: Use the official Laravel Docker image or build your own.
Add a managed Postgres database to the same Render project.

hostingOptions.name: VPS (Ubuntu + nginx + PHP-FPM)
notes: Use Laravel Deployer or Capistrano for zero-downtime releases.
Configure Supervisor to keep queue workers running.
Set up a cron job: `* * * * * php /var/www/html/artisan schedule:run`.

buildConfig.buildCommand: php artisan key:generate --force && php artisan config:cache && php artisan route:cache && php artisan view:cache && php artisan optimize && npm ci && npm run build

envVars.name: APP_KEY
envVars.description: Laravel application encryption key — generate with: php artisan key:generate --show

envVars.name: APP_URL
envVars.description: Full URL of your production app

envVars.name: CACHE_DRIVER
envVars.description: Cache backend: file, redis, memcached, database

buildSteps: RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor curl unzip
RUN docker-php-ext-install pdo pdo_mysql pdo_pgsql opcache
dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
FROM php:8.3-fpm-alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor
RUN docker-php-ext-install pdo pdo_mysql opcache
COPY --from=composer:2 /usr/bin/composer /usr/bin/composer
WORKDIR /var/www/html
COPY composer.json composer.lock ./
RUN composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader
COPY . .
RUN php artisan optimize
RUN chown -R www-data:www-data storage bootstrap/cache
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["/usr/bin/supervisord", "-n"]

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy Laravel App
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  test-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: shivammathur/setup-php@v2
        with:
          php-version: '8.3'
          extensions: pdo, pdo_mysql, mbstring, xml, bcmath
      - run: composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader
      - run: cp .env.example .env && php artisan key:generate
      - run: php artisan test
      # Add your deploy step: ssh + php artisan migrate --force, or vapor deploy

checklist: Generate APP_KEY with `php artisan key:generate --show` and store it as a secret
Run `php artisan migrate --force` as part of your deploy script
Enable OPcache in php.ini for 3–5× performance improvement
Run `php artisan optimize` (config:cache, route:cache, view:cache) after every deploy
Configure Supervisor to keep queue workers (`php artisan queue:work`) alive
Set up a cron job for the Laravel task scheduler
Point nginx `root` to the `/public` directory — never expose the project root
Set file permissions: `chmod -R 775 storage bootstrap/cache`

wordpress.summary: WordPress site — managed WordPress hosts (WP Engine, Kinsta) eliminate server management. Docker with the official image enables self-hosting. WP-CLI automates plugin, theme, and migration tasks.

hostingOptions.name: WP Engine
notes: Fully managed: automated backups, updates, and security scanning.
Deploy via Git push or SFTP — WP Engine has a native Git integration.
Built-in CDN, staging environments, and one-click site cloning.

hostingOptions.name: Kinsta
notes: Google Cloud-based with 37 global data centers.
Automated daily backups and free SSL.
DevKinsta local dev tool mirrors your production environment.

hostingOptions.name: SiteGround
notes: Budget-friendly shared hosting with a WordPress-optimized stack.
One-click WordPress install via cPanel.
SG Optimizer plugin for caching and CDN.

hostingOptions.name: Cloudways
notes: Managed cloud hosting — pick your underlying provider.
Deploy from GitHub with the Git deployment add-on.
Bot protection, malware scanning, and automated backups included.

hostingOptions.name: Docker (Self-Hosted)
notes: Use the official wordpress Docker image.
Persist wp-content via a named Docker volume.
Use Traefik or nginx-proxy for TLS termination.

buildConfig.buildCommand: wp core download && wp core install

envVars.name: WORDPRESS_DEBUG
envVars.description: Enable debug logging — set to false in production

dockerConfig.dockerfileSnippet:
# docker-compose.yml (recommended for WordPress)
version: '3.8'
services:
  wordpress:
    image: wordpress:php8.3-apache
    ports: ['80:80']
    environment:
      WORDPRESS_DB_HOST: db
      WORDPRESS_DB_NAME: wordpress
      WORDPRESS_DB_USER: wordpress
      WORDPRESS_DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - wp_content:/var/www/html/wp-content
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: wordpress
      MYSQL_USER: wordpress
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/mysql
volumes: {wp_content: {}, db_data: {}}

ciConfig.content:
name: Deploy WordPress
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Sync theme/plugin files via rsync
        run: |
          rsync -avz --delete wp-content/ user@your-server:/var/www/html/wp-content/
      - name: Run database migrations (if using WP-CLI)
        run: |
          ssh user@your-server 'wp db optimize && wp cache flush'
      # Or: use WP Engine / Kinsta Git push deploy

checklist: Generate fresh secret keys from https://api.wordpress.org/secret-key/1.1/salt/ and store them securely
Set WORDPRESS_DEBUG=false and WP_DEBUG=false in production wp-config.php
Install a caching plugin (WP Super Cache or W3 Total Cache) for static-file caching
Enable a CDN (Cloudflare or your host's built-in CDN) for assets
Back up the database and wp-content before every major update
Limit login attempts — install a security plugin (Wordfence, Solid Security)
Disable file editing in wp-admin: add `define('DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT', true)` to wp-config.php
Use a staging environment to test plugin and core updates before applying to production
